title: TrustLens evidence record
description: One record per component run. Every TrustLens component emits records conforming to this schema, so that static, configured and dynamic evidence about the same artifact can be compared without a translation layer.

record_id.description: Identifies this particular run: first 32 hex of SHA-256 over (content_hash || run.started_at). Distinct from content_hash — two runs producing identical evidence share a content_hash but not a record_id. Derivation in SCHEMA.md.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 over the canonical body — the record with record_id, content_hash and run.completed_at removed, keys sorted, UTF-8, no whitespace. Two runs producing identical evidence produce identical content_hash.

execution_mode.description: `dry_run` performs no analysis and no acquisition — it reports what WOULD be fetched and analysed. It is the default for any operation with a remote or executing component.

invocation.description: Argv used, so the run can be repeated.

config_hash.description: SHA-256 of the configuration file used, or null if none.

reasoning_notes.description: Chronological trace of what the run did, including every decision to skip, exclude or abort. Read by a human reconstructing why a scope ended where it did.

scope.description: Record-level scope: the union across all checks. Per-check scope lives on each finding and may be narrower.

input_records.description: Records this one was composed from. Empty for a component that reads an artifact directly; non-empty for any component that combines earlier evidence. A finding, contradiction or mitigation may reference an identifier belonging to one of these records; without a declared input, an unresolvable reference is an error rather than an external reference.

declared_capabilities.description: What the artifact SAYS about itself. Declarations are recorded verbatim with their location. An artifact that declares nothing produces an empty array AND a corresponding unknown — never an assumed-safe default.

declaration.description: What the artifact claims about this capability. `explicitly_absent` means the artifact states it does not do this; `not_required` means it is not listed among requirements. These are different claims.

verbatim.description: The exact text supporting this declaration. Required so a reader can check TrustLens's reading of the document against the document.

contradictions.description: Conflicts between declared, configured, static and dynamic evidence. Recorded, never silently reconciled. A contradiction is a finding about the evidence set, not an error to be cleaned up.

ref.description: finding_id, or an index into declared_capabilities.

reconciled.description: Always false in a machine-produced record. TrustLens does not resolve contradictions between supplied inputs; a human does, outside the record.

mitigations.description: Finding-specific mitigations. Every entry names the findings that triggered it and the path it is expected to remove.

environment_description_ref.description: Present when the mitigation targets a resource known only from an environment description, so its capture time is shown alongside the recommendation.

generic_advice.description: Non-specific guidance. Permitted only alongside at least one specific mitigation — enforced below — so that generic advice can never stand in for finding-specific output.

residual_uncertainty.description: Prose statement of what remains unresolved after this run. Required and non-empty on every record: there is no run of any TrustLens component that leaves nothing unresolved.

environment_description_ref.description: The environment description this whole run was based on, when applicable. Required non-null for credential_mapper records.

sandbox_status.description: EXPERIMENTAL until a validated review record authorises a specific profile. Recorded in the evidence, not only in documentation.

timeout_seconds.description: Integer, not float: the canonical hash rejects floating-point values so that record hashes are reproducible across languages and platforms.

banner.description: Machine-readable warning banner. Must be present in every sandbox record and rendered in every downstream report.

allOf.$comment: An EXPERIMENTAL sandbox must carry the warning banner verbatim, in the evidence record itself.

banner.const: EXPERIMENTAL — DO NOT USE FOR SUSPECTED ZERO-DAY OR HOSTILE ARTIFACTS

allOf.$comment: REVIEWED requires a review record hash and at least one approved profile; documentation alone cannot produce this state.

claims.description: Machine-readable statement of what this record does and does not establish, carried with the evidence so that the boundary travels with the data rather than living only in a document a reader may not open.

allOf.$comment: LOAD-BEARING. Generic advice may accompany specific mitigations but may never stand in for them.
A credential-mapper record without a dated environment description would have no way to show its own staleness.
A blast-radius record is by definition a composition, so it must name the records it composed. A simulation with no declared inputs has no traceable evidence base.
A sandbox record must carry its sandbox block; nothing else may.
Only a sandbox run may claim sandboxed_execution mode.
